mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between INDPRO and PAYEMS shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 20d returns are negative when both series rise by >1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand-side momentum with contained price pressures, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between HOUST and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 20d returns are negative when HOUST rises >2% and CPIAUCSL falls >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) in a low-unemployment environment (UNRATE < 3.5%) signals sustained demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between RSAFS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 60d returns are negative when RSAFS rises >1% and UNRATE < 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor market dynamics that boost corporate pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4%) indicates supply-side weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 60d returns are positive when INDPRO falls >1% and FEDFUNDS > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth (GDPC1) in a high-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS > 4%) signals economic stagnation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS shows Rank IC < 0.03, or 60d returns are positive when GDPC1 falls >0.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4%.
